description_long: This object is a vintage-style camera mounted on a sturdy three-legged tripod. The camera body is boxy and features a brown and gold color scheme, with a prominent lens assembly at the front and a bellows section connecting the lens to the main body. A large, round flashbulb is attached to the top left of the camera, supported by a metallic arm. The tripod legs are metallic and splay outward for stability, with a crank handle visible on the side of the camera for manual operation. The camera has side handles and a top-mounted viewfinder, giving it a classic, early 20th-century appearance. The overall construction suggests a combination of metal and wood, with some plastic or glass elements for the lens and flash.
description: A vintage box camera with a bellows and large flash, mounted on a sturdy three-legged metal tripod. The camera features a brown and gold body, side handles, and a crank handle for manual operation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the camera lens, bellows, and large flashbulb above the lens, with all three tripod legs visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the bellows extension, side handle, and the metallic arm supporting the flashbulb.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the camera, the crank handle, and the tripod's leg arrangement.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the bellows, side handle, and the flashbulb's mounting arm from a different angle.
